title: Piptomeris odontoclada
intro: Piptomeris odontoclada, commonly known as Toothed Piptomeris, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Piptomeris odontoclada include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Piptomeris odontoclada is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the preparation of tinctures and extracts.

This classification places Piptomeris odontoclada within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The kingdom Plantae is itself a subset of the broader domain Eukarya, which encompasses all eukaryotic organisms. At the highest taxonomic level, Piptomeris odontoclada is part of the domain Eukarya.
